verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II membrane protein with an N-terminal TM domain that anchors it to membranes. While its baseline/canonical localization is trans-Golgi, it has a major documented pool at the plasma membrane where it functions as a recognition molecule in cell-cell and cell-matrix interactions. The protein reaches the outer face via its own TM anchor mechanism (type II topology with N-terminal hydrophobic signal sequence), not via transient recruitment. The surface form is stable and has documented biological function.
